title: Webhooks, Replay Defense, SSRF, Unsafe Consumption, and Business Abuse · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for webhooks, replay defense, ssrf, unsafe consumption, and business abuse and demands the smallest safe signed ingress, bounded egress, and abuse decision model with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for signed ingress, bounded egress, and abuse decision model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Verify provider identity, algorithm, exact bytes, timestamp freshness, constant-behavior signature comparison, key rotation, and failure disclosure before parsing.
Deduplicate stable delivery identifiers, persist acceptance atomically, return promptly, process asynchronously, and classify retryable provider responses.
Allowlist scheme and port, canonicalize host, resolve and revalidate every destination, block private or metadata ranges, control redirects, and enforce egress network policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Webhook Signature Freshness · changed case · Quiz Webhook Signature Freshne…
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz team handles case 2733 in a privacy-sensitive records API using conditional requests. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine signed ingress freshness, replay defense, and SSRF egress policy; record only bounded redacted diagnostic fields; then change the method and preserve both status and durable-effect evidence. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Verify provider identity, algorithm, exact bytes, timestamp freshness, constant-behavior signature comparison, key rotation, and failure disclosure before parsing.
